zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Alice Moore於1251年出生在英國戴文德利時，她的父親John Moore31歲，母親Sarah30歲。她於1281年與John Chudleigh在英國英格蘭戴文德利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1289年在英國享年38歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Alice Moore于1251年出生在英国戴文德利时，她的父亲John Moore31岁，母亲Sarah30岁。她于1281年与John Chudleigh在英国英格兰戴文德利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1289年在英国享年38岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: 1  Scottish, English:  see  Moore  .

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Alice Moore was born in 1251, in Chudleigh, Devon, England, her father, John Moore, was 31 and her mother, Sarah, was 30. She married John Chudleigh in 1281, in Chudleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1289, in England, at the age of 38.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Alice Moore est née en 1251, à Chudleigh, Devon, Angleterre, son père, John Moore, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Sarah, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John Chudleigh en 1281, à Chudleigh, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1289, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Alice Moore 1251 in Chudleigh, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Moore 31 und ihre Mutter Sarah 30. Sie hat 1281 in Chudleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom John Chudleigh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1289 im Alter von 38 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Alice Moore è nata il nel 1251 nel Chudleigh, Devon, England, sua padre, John Moore, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Sarah, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato John Chudleigh nel 1281, nel Chudleigh, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1289, nel England, all'età di 38 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Mooreが1251年にイギリスデボンチウドレイグで生まれた時，父John Mooreは31歳，母Sarahは30歳であった。1281年にイギリスイギリスデボンチウドレイグでJohn Chudleighと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1289年にイギリスで38歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Moore(이)가 1251년 영국 데폰 c후들리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Moore(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1281년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 c후들리에서 John Chudleigh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1289년 영국에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Alice Moore nasceu em 1251, em Chudleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Moore, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Sarah, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com John Chudleigh em 1281, em Chudleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1289, em Inglaterra, com 38 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Alice Moore родилась в 1251 г. в местности чудлиг, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени John Moore было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Sarah, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Chudleigh в 1281 г. в местности чудлиг, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1289 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 38 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Alice Moore nació en 1251, en Chudleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, John Moore, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Sarah, tenía 30 años. Se casó con John Chudleigh en 1281, en Chudleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1289, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años.
